about.title: About Us
about.intro: With over 18 years of experience in civil engineering, we help each client plan and deliver practical, code-compliant and cost-effective infrastructure solutions.

capabilities.aqueduct: Integrated design for aqueduct, sewer, and storm drainage infrastructure
capabilities.hydrology: Hydrology and hydraulic engineering for road and urban development projects
capabilities.grading: Practical grading and site-servicing solutions focused on constructability

hero.title: Powerful rainfall insights for North American engineers and planners
hero.subtitle: Search Environment Canada stations and U.S. NOAA Atlas 14 points, access IDF curves, and share reports with your team in seconds. Built for civil engineers, hydrologists, and municipal planning departments.

preview.label: Live preview
preview.title: Mission, BC — 100 year return period peak intensity
preview.subtitle: Retrieve accurate IDF curves across Canada and the U.S. in just one click.

features.title: All the tools you need for rainfall design on one screen
features.subtitle: Built for civil engineers, water resource specialists, hydrologists, and municipal planners who need quick, defensible design rainfall data.

smartLookup.title: Smart Station Lookup
smartLookup.description: Search Canadian stations and U.S. locations by municipality, postal code, or GPS coordinates.

instantCurves.title: Instant IDF Curves
instantCurves.description: Generate intensity-duration-frequency charts in both metric and imperial units, ready for design work.

shareable.title: Shareable Deliverables
shareable.description: Export reports for plan submissions, consultant reviews, and GIS collaborators in a single click.

cta.title: Deliver rainfall data with confidence every time
cta.subtitle: Rainfall Data Finder aggregates trusted Canadian and U.S. rainfall datasets and gives your engineering team a single place to retrieve up-to-date IDF curves, station metadata, and ready-to-share reports.

pricing.title: Choose the plan that fits your team
pricing.subtitle: Simple subscription tiers for individual consultants, engineering firms, and municipal departments. Cancel anytime.

trial.name: 7-day trial
trial.description: Verify your card with a refundable $1 charge, then start a 7-day trial. After trial, membership auto-renews at $30/month unless canceled before renewal.

perks.autoRenew: Auto-renews at $30/month after trial unless canceled

consultantMonthly.name: Consultant Monthly
consultantMonthly.description: Unlimited station lookups, IDF curves, and PDF exports.

lifetime.name: Lifetime Membership (limited-time)
lifetime.description: Lifetime access for the first 300 purchasers. Conditions apply.

trial.title: Trial terms
trial.body:
A valid credit/debit card is required to start the 7-day trial. A temporary $1 verification charge is applied and refunded after successful verification.

By starting the trial, you authorize automatic renewal to the Consultant Monthly plan at $30/month when the trial ends, unless canceled before renewal.

Trial access is limited to one per email/account. We may decline or revoke trial access for suspected abuse or fraud.

lifetime.title: Lifetime terms
lifetime.body:
Lifetime membership is a one-time purchase that grants access to the Civispec application for one account, for as long as the service is offered.

Offer is limited to the first 300 completed purchases and may be modified or ended at any time before purchase.

Membership is non-transferable and intended for a single account/user. Access may be suspended for violations of our Terms of Service or suspected fraud. Taxes may apply. Refunds are subject to our refund policy.

viewer.title: IDF Viewer
viewer.intro: Enter a location to find the nearest weather station and its intensity-duration-frequency (IDF) curves.

country.label: Country
country.usComingSoon: United States NOAA Atlas 14 provider is available.
country.usNeedsCoordinates: Enter U.S. latitude/longitude or a U.S. location name to load NOAA Atlas 14 IDF values.
country.usNeedsInput: Enter either a U.S. location name or valid latitude/longitude.
country.invalidCoordinates: Please enter valid U.S. coordinates (latitude -90..90, longitude -180..180).
country.usFetchFailed: NOAA request failed. Please try again in a moment.
country.usNoDataYet: US provider is connected, but no curve data is available yet.

usForm.helper: Use a U.S. place name or decimal coordinates (example: Columbia, SC or 33.8000, -81.0000).
usForm.placePlaceholder: e.g., Columbia, SC or ZIP code
usForm.placeHint: Type and pick from the dropdown to auto-fill coordinates, or keep typing and we will geocode on load.
usForm.unitsNote: US curves are displayed in imperial units ({{units}}).

authPrompt.suffix: to access IDF curves and tables.

climate.canadaHint: Uses IDF_CC factors when available for the selected station, otherwise the nearest station with factors.

fallback.toggleLabelSuffix: data, use the nearest station for short durations
fallback.hint: Some ECCC stations have missing sub-hour data; enabling this keeps your table/plot on the standard short-duration grid.

services.title: Our Services
services.intro: Practical, code-compliant civil and structural engineering for small projects, renovations, and site development. Clear drawings, constructible details, and responsive support through permitting and construction.

structural.title: Structural design
structural.description: Design of wood, steel, and reinforced concrete components for safe load paths and efficient framing. Typical deliverables include foundation sizing, beam and lintel design, connection detailing, and notes aligned with the applicable building code.

permit.title: Permit drawings
permit.description: Permit-ready drawing sets that help reviewers quickly understand the scope and compliance approach. We focus on clean sheets, clear notes, and coordination between architectural intent and engineering details.

bullets.notesDetails: General notes, typical details, and schedules
bullets.coordination: Coordination with site and grading drawings
bullets.revisions: Revisions and responses during plan review

grading.title: Grading & drainage plans
grading.description: Site grading concepts focused on constructability, positive drainage, and sensible stormwater routing. Where needed, we can provide rational-method sizing checks and supporting documentation for approvals.

bullets.spotElevations: Spot elevations, slopes, swales, and flow arrows
bullets.catchBasins: Catch basins / culverts / driveway drainage concepts
bullets.erosionControl: Erosion and sediment control notes (as applicable)

sitePlanning.title: Site planning & analysis
sitePlanning.description: Early feasibility input to reduce redesign: layout constraints, drainage paths, grading implications, and practical construction sequencing considerations.

cta.title: Have a project in mind?
cta.description: Tell us your project scope, location, and timeline. We can review your needs and suggest the right service package.

errors.invalidCredentials: Unable to sign in. Please verify your credentials.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.shortNotice: This tool is for information and planning support. Always verify values against official sources and local requirements before design or construction decisions.
disclaimer.acknowledgement: I understand these results are informational only, may include uncertainty, and must be independently verified before use in design, permitting, construction, or safety-related decisions.

purpose.title: Purpose of this tool
purpose.body: This app helps users view rainfall and IDF information across Canada and the United States for planning and early design support. It does not replace professional engineering judgment.

sources.title: Data sources
sources.body: Data is sourced from official public publications (including Environment and Climate Change Canada and NOAA Atlas 14 in the United States) and may also rely on third-party services such as geocoding or mapping providers.

processing.title: How results are produced
processing.body: To generate results, the app may apply station matching, formatting/cleaning, unit conversion, interpolation, and climate-adjustment logic. Because of these steps, outputs may differ from source tables.

uncertainty.title: Limits and uncertainty
uncertainty.body: Outputs may include errors, omissions, outdated records, station metadata issues, fallback station use, and model uncertainty (especially climate-adjusted scenarios).

userResponsibility.title: Your responsibility
userResponsibility.body: You are responsible for independently validating all values against official publications, confirming code/municipal requirements, and obtaining qualified professional review where required.

noWarranty.title: No warranty
noWarranty.body: This service is provided "as is" and "as available" without guarantees of accuracy, completeness, fitness for purpose, or uninterrupted availability.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for losses, delays, costs, damages, or claims arising from use of this app or reliance on its outputs.

criticalUse.title: Not for sole use in critical decisions
criticalUse.body: Do not use this app as the only basis for life-safety, emergency, or critical infrastructure decisions.

changes.title: Updates and changes
changes.body: Data, methods, station mappings, and outputs may change over time without notice.

contact.title: Questions
contact.body: If you have questions about this disclaimer, contact support.

disclaimer.exportFooter: Generated by CiviSpec for informational use only. Verify against official publications and local requirements before application.
